headline: International police recovered 27 million stolen credentials in an Operation Endgame strike on StealC and Amadey as fresh supply-chain and network-device flaws came under active attack.

emerging_trends.text: Software supply-chain attacks dominated the day, with Cordyceps hijacking CI/CD pipelines at Microsoft, Google, and Apache while stolen Klue OAuth tokens drained Salesforce data from LastPass and BeyondTrust.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Cordyceps CI/CD Flaws Threaten Open-Source Supply Chain

emerging_trends.text: The criminal supply line feeding ransomware drew heavy fire as Operation Endgame gutted StealC and Amadey while the Mistic backdoor kept handing Qilin and Akira fresh access.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Operation Endgame Recovers 27 Million Stolen Credentials

emerging_trends.text: Attackers turned AI into both weapon and obstacle, slipping malicious skills into ClawHub and lacing spyware with forbidden text to defeat automated analysis.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Attackers Weaponize and Poison AI Tools

topics.title: Operation Endgame Recovers 27 Million Stolen Credentials
topics.latest_developments: Law enforcement and partners Bitdefender, Bitsight, ESET, Microsoft, and Proofpoint recovered 27 million stolen credentials after disrupting hundreds of command-and-control servers behind StealC and Amadey on June 24, 2026.
topics.summary: StealC and Amadey are infostealer and loader malware that work in tandem to compromise devices, harvest data, and deliver ransomware and financial fraud; Microsoft's Digital Crimes Unit and Europol seized and blocked the domains forming their backbone. Affected users should change exposed passwords.

sources.title: Amadey and StealC Malware Network Disrupted, 27M Stolen Credentials Recovered
StealC and Amadey: Breaking down infostealers and the cybercrime services that deliver them
Amadey, StealC malware operations disrupted in Operation Endgame action
Law enforcement hits StealC and Amadey malware networks

topics.title: Attackers Weaponize and Poison AI Tools
topics.latest_developments: OpenClaw pulled five infostealer-laden skills from its ClawHub marketplace, and Bruce Schneier reported a malware author embedding fake nuclear and biological weapons text in spyware comments to trip safety filters and block automated AI analysis.
topics.summary: Adversaries now treat AI agents, their marketplaces, and the data they read as an attack surface, planting malicious skills and poisoning inputs that autonomous systems trust. Teams should vet agent skills and avoid relying solely on AI for malware triage.

sources.title: More Malicious OpenClaw Skills Threaten AI Supply Chain
OpenClaw's Skill Marketplace and the Emerging AI Supply Chain Threat
Embedding Forbidden Text in Spyware to Discourage AI Analysis
When Information Becomes the Attack Surface – Understanding AI Agent Traps

topics.title: CISA Flags Ubiquiti and Lantronix Flaws Under Attack
topics.latest_developments: CISA detailed CVE-2025-67038, a CVSS 9.8 code-injection flaw in Lantronix EDS5000 serial-to-ethernet servers under active exploitation, and ordered federal civilian agencies to patch it alongside the Ubiquiti UniFi OS flaws by June 26, 2026.
topics.summary: The flaws let remote, unauthenticated attackers change system settings, reach underlying accounts, and inject commands on widely deployed network gear. Operators should apply the Ubiquiti and Lantronix fixes immediately.

sources.title: CISA Warns Critical Lantronix EDS5000 Flaw Is Being Actively Exploited
CISA warns of max severity Ubiquiti flaws exploited in attacks
Critical Ubiquiti Vulnerabilities in Attackers' Crosshairs

topics.title: Klue Salesforce Breach Engulfs LastPass and BeyondTrust
topics.latest_developments: LastPass and BeyondTrust confirmed attackers used OAuth tokens stolen in the Klue supply-chain breach to take their Salesforce data, joining over a dozen confirmed victims as the Icarus extortion group leaked stolen files.
topics.summary: Klue, a market-intelligence platform integrated with Salesforce and Gong across sales teams, lost OAuth tokens that attackers reused to reach customer CRM data. Affected firms should revoke Klue tokens and review Salesforce access logs.

sources.title: BeyondTrust, LastPass Impacted by Klue-Salesforce Incident
LastPass customer data exposed through Klue supply chain attack
Scope of Salesforce Attacks Expands as Icarus Leaks Data

topics.title: Mistic Backdoor Feeds Qilin and Akira Ransomware
topics.latest_developments: Researchers exposed Mistic, a stealthy backdoor that initial access broker Woodgnat, tracked as KongTuke, uses to open doors for Qilin, Interlock, Rhysida, Akira, 8Base, and Black Basta ransomware.
topics.summary: Mistic targets insurance, education, IT, and professional-services organizations in financially motivated attacks that precede ransomware deployment. Defenders should hunt for the backdoor and monitor for follow-on ransomware staging.

sources.title: New 'Mistic' RAT Opens Door to Several Ransomware Families
Stealthy Mistic backdoor linked to ransomware access broker KongTuke

topics.title: Cordyceps CI/CD Flaws Threaten Open-Source Supply Chain
topics.latest_developments: Novee Security disclosed Cordyceps, a class of CI/CD workflow weakness that lets unauthenticated attackers hijack workflows and seize control of more than 300 GitHub repositories at Microsoft, Google, and Apache.
topics.summary: Cordyceps abuses misconfigured continuous-integration pipelines to compromise the open-source software supply chain, exposing millions of downstream repositories to hijacking. Maintainers should audit workflow triggers and restrict pipeline permissions.

sources.title: Cordyceps CI/CD Flaws Expose 300+ GitHub Repositories to Supply-Chain Attacks
Exploitable CI/CD Vulnerabilities Expose Millions of Repositories to Hijacking

weather: Thursday: Partly Sunny then Scattered Showers And Thunderstorms, high 84F.

games.recap: Cole Young hits a two-run home run in the seventh to lift Mariners to a 3-2 victory over Pirates

business_politics.title: War Premium Drains Out of Markets
business_politics.latest_developments: Oil settled near pre-war levels Wednesday, falling to its lowest since the U.S.-Iran conflict began as more tankers cleared the Persian Gulf, while gold sank to a November low and bitcoin slid below $60,000.
business_politics.summary: Crude fell about 4% to its lowest since the start of the U.S.-Iran war, Treasury yields eased, gold dropped 3.4% and silver 6.4%, and bitcoin hit a 20-month low under $60,000 as cooling Middle East tension and a rotation toward AI stocks pulled the safe-haven and geopolitical bid out of commodities and crypto.

sources.title: Oil Futures Fall to Lowest Since the Outbreak of War
Gold Tumbles to November Low
Bitcoin hits 20-month low as market sentiment sours
Treasury Yields Fall Ahead of U.S. Activity, Inflation Data

business_politics.title: Trump Holds Housing Bill Hostage to Voter Law
business_politics.latest_developments: President Trump abruptly canceled Wednesday's signing of the bipartisan housing-affordability bill, blindsiding Senate Republicans and vowing to withhold his signature until Congress passes his SAVE America voter-eligibility measure.
business_politics.summary: President Trump shelved a bipartisan bill meant to spur home construction and curb institutional investors in housing, dismissing it as of "minor importance" and tying his signature to passage of legislation requiring proof of citizenship to vote.

sources.title: Trump abruptly cancels signing ceremony for bipartisan housing bill
Trump scraps signing of US housing affordability bill

business.title: City Council Passes Skill-Games Tax
business.latest_developments: Pittsburgh City Council voted Wednesday to tax skill-game terminals, enacting the levy Councilman Anthony Coghill floated a week earlier, though collection remains uncertain until Harrisburg acts.
business.summary: Pittsburgh City Council approved a tax on skill-game machines after the Pennsylvania Supreme Court classified them as slot machines, but whether the city can collect it hinges on the regulations or levies the state ultimately imposes.

sources.title: Pittsburgh council gambles on skill games tax by voting in favor of levy
City Council passes tax on gaming terminals, but doubts remain over whether they can collect

business.title: CMU, UPMC Win Up to $39.3M for Fetal Monitoring
business.latest_developments: A Pittsburgh team led by Carnegie Mellon University and UPMC Magee-Womens will share a grant of up to $39.3 million to improve fetal monitoring.
business.summary: Carnegie Mellon University and UPMC Magee-Womens Hospital will split federal funding of up to $39.3 million to develop better technology for monitoring fetal health.

sources.title: Pittsburgh team to share up to $39.3 million grant, aiming to improve fetal monitoring

business.title: Pirates Open Legacy Hall at PNC Park
business.latest_developments: The Pittsburgh Pirates announced the opening of Legacy Hall, a new event and meeting space at PNC Park holding the ballpark's largest display of team memorabilia.
business.summary: The Pittsburgh Pirates opened Legacy Hall at PNC Park, an event and meeting venue showcasing the largest collection of Pirates memorabilia at the ballpark.

sources.title: Legacy Hall at PNC Park: A unique event space with Pittsburgh Pirates history on display

around_town.title: Pittsburgh Offers Free Kids' Swim Lessons
around_town.latest_developments: The City of Pittsburgh opened free summer swimming lessons for children ages 6 to 15.
around_town.summary: The City of Pittsburgh is providing free swimming lessons for kids ages 6 to 15 at its public pools this summer.

sources.title: City of Pittsburgh offers free swimming lessons for kids

around_town.title: Point Park Adds Transfer Scholarship
around_town.latest_developments: Point Park University will give transfer students a $3,000 scholarship this fall to draw them to its Downtown Pittsburgh campus.
around_town.summary: Point Park University introduced a $3,000 scholarship for transfer students starting this fall, betting it can sway them into enrolling at the Downtown college.

sources.title: Point Park giving $3K scholarship to transfer students this fall

around_town.title: Slippery Rock Plans Construction Degree
around_town.latest_developments: Slippery Rock University proposed a bachelor's degree in construction management aimed at working trade professionals.
around_town.summary: Slippery Rock University officials proposed a construction-management bachelor's degree to give skilled trade workers a pathway to a four-year credential.

sources.title: Slippery Rock proposes construction management degree to trade workers

events.title: Pittsburgh Choreography Festival at Charity Randall
events.latest_developments: The Pittsburgh Dance Workshop and Choreography Festival runs Thursday through Saturday, June 25 to 27, at the Charity Randall Theatre in Oakland.
events.summary: The Pittsburgh Dance Workshop and Choreography Festival, gathering emerging and established choreographers to present original works, plays at various times Thursday, June 25, Friday, June 26, and Saturday, June 27, at the Charity Randall Theatre in Oakland.

sources.title: 14+ things to do this weekend, from the Andy Warhol Bridge 100th Birthday Bash to OpenStreetPGH

events.title: Nelly, Third Eye Blind Concert Sells Out
events.latest_developments: Tickets to America250PA's free Point State Park concert Saturday, June 27, have sold out, and road closures and parking restrictions around Downtown have already begun.
events.summary: America250PA's free Commonwealth Concert at Point State Park Downtown on Saturday, June 27, headlined by Nelly and Third Eye Blind with Pittsburgh rapper Frzy, has reached capacity, triggering road closures and parking restrictions across Downtown.

sources.title: Road closures have begun in advance of America250PA concert with Nelly, Third Eye Blind

around_teams.title: Hiles: Pirates Shouldn't Buy at the Deadline
around_teams.latest_developments: Post-Gazette columnist Noah Hiles argued the Pirates have not earned the right to add at the MLB trade deadline, putting the stakes on Ben Cherington, Paul Skenes, and owner Bob Nutting.
around_teams.summary: In a June 24 column, Post-Gazette writer Noah Hiles contended the Pirates' play makes them undeserving of buying at the trade deadline, framing the franchise's choices around general manager Ben Cherington, ace Paul Skenes, and owner Bob Nutting.

sources.title: Noah Hiles: These Pirates don't deserve to be buyers at the MLB trade deadline

around_teams.title: Film Study on Rookie Robert Spears-Jennings
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette film breakdown praised Steelers rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings, the Oklahoma product, for his motor and his special-teams value.
around_teams.summary: The Post-Gazette's film study of Steelers rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings, drafted out of Oklahoma, highlighted his effort against the run and the role he projects to fill on special teams.

sources.title: Steelers film study: Rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings brings effort, special teams value

around_teams.title: Eric Ebron on Not Just Football
around_teams.latest_developments: Former tight end Eric Ebron joined Cam Heyward's Not Just Football to discuss his NFL journey and his retirement at 28.
around_teams.summary: On Not Just Football with Cam Heyward, ex-tight end Eric Ebron walked through his stints in Detroit, Indianapolis, and Pittsburgh, his early retirement, and Bill Belichick's move to coach North Carolina.

sources.source: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
sources.title: Eric Ebron Talks Andrew Luck's Retirement, The 11-0 Steelers Team, & More! | Not Just Football

team_usa.title: Pulisic Hopes to Play vs Türkiye
team_usa.latest_developments: Christian Pulisic said he is ready and hoping to play in the USMNT's Group D finale against Türkiye on Thursday, June 25, though he is "probably not ready" for a full 90 minutes after his calf injury.
team_usa.summary: United States captain Christian Pulisic, recovered from the left calf injury that sidelined him against Australia, said he hopes to feature against Türkiye on Thursday in the U.S. men's already-clinched final group match, while doubting he can last the full 90 minutes.

sources.title: Pulisic ready, 'hoping to play' for U.S. vs. Türkiye

team_usa.title: Diggins, Olympians Lobby Congress on Climate
team_usa.latest_developments: Olympic cross-country skier Jessie Diggins and the athlete group Protect Our Winters visited Capitol Hill this week to press lawmakers on climate policy amid concern over Environmental Protection Agency moves.
team_usa.summary: Olympic gold-medal skier Jessie Diggins joined the athlete-led group Protect Our Winters at the U.S. Capitol to push Congress for climate solutions, citing worries about EPA rollbacks that threaten winter sports.

sources.title: Olympians push for climate change solutions

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: My Vibe Coding Adventure, The App and the Experience, Ten Takeaways
reading.summary: Ben Thompson recounts vibe-coding an app he plans to use regularly and draws ten takeaways about what building software with AI tools is actually like.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Cargo Culture
reading.summary: Zitron argues the tech industry is engaged in cargo-cult behavior, mimicking the trappings of AI success without the underlying substance or economics to sustain it.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport criticizes AI firms for publicizing alarming warnings about their own products, likening it to Ford issuing a whitepaper fretting over the dangers of its best-selling F-150.
